--- Page 2 ---
A WORD FROM THE EXPERTS
In case of relapse during phases 2 or 3 of the CDED, a return to the previous phase will be necessary. At the end of the CDED, appropriate nutritional care is recommended. A transition to a more diversified diet, excluding ultra-processed foods, can be considered with appropriate dietetic follow-up.
A WORD FROM THE EXPERTS
The CDED is not suitable for all patients. Its implementation requires a medical prescription and personalized follow-up by your gastroenterologist as well as a specialized dietitian.
The CDED can be used alone or in combination with medical treatment.
What is the CDED ?
What is the CDED ?
The exclusion of certain foods, replaced by artificial nutrition (oral or by tube), could allow induction of remission and healing in Crohn’s disease1. Exclusive Enteral Nutrition (EEN) is nevertheless difficult to follow for several weeks.
